video_title: Asterix & Obelix: The Big Fight | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I ve finally watched the Asterix mini series at Netflix that was in development for a long time. Unfortunately just like the Super Mario Bros movie it was a mixed bag.

The good parts:
- The main iconic characters, the artstyle and generally the spirit of the show are faithful to the franchise and it has some great references to the comics and even the first Asterix cartoon movies.
- The visuals, the animation and the fights  while obviously aren't as good as the 3d animated movies that were released in the past at least they're quite good. especially for netflix standards.

The bad parts:
- It really shows that it's a Netflix show. While it's not fully woken like most Netflix adaptions it has some parts that scream "Netflix" and "Blackrock". Like some of the new characters that are Blackrock-friendly and so out of place for Asterix, both from design and personality aspect. They even replaced a druid from the Asterix comics (all druids at the comics were male, just like in real life)...with a female feminist-looking witch. Also no black character with the iconic caricature style of the comics and the animated movies since they re "too racist" for Netflix standards  Instead they shoved a black female character that is Blackrock-friendly both from design and personality aspect. And one of the villains who s a new character was sooooo out of place for an Asterix character (both from design and personality aspect) with him being a cringe racist "Romans supremacist" strawman stereotype that was like he was written by American leftist writers. 

In conclusion:
Does it worth watching it? My answer is: Only if you re a hardcore Asterix fan like a man. If you re not, skip the show and watch some of the old superior movies (including the 3d animated ones) and of course the comics.

video_title: Asterix & Obelix: The Big Fight | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
Bon bah après trois épisodes, c'est encore mieux que dans mes rêves les plus fous. Tout est "parfait". Visuellement, c'est un menhir reçu en pleine tronche. C'est coloré, vivant, rond, facétieux. La forêt, la grotte de la Goth, la neige, ... Jamais je n'avais eu cette sensation de BD qui prend chair sous mes yeux, comme si je mettais un pied puis l'autre dans les cases de BD.

Le soin apporté aux textures des décors, à la patine des objets est bluffant. C'est organique, chaleureux, chatoyant.

Concernant l'humour, rien à redire, c'est du Chabat (pas du Goscinny ! chacun est différent mais personne n'est irremplaçable) mais par Belenos, que ça passe mieux dans un Astérix en animation.

Concernant les voix françaises, donc originales, il faut un petit temps d'adaptation. Non pas qu'elles soient mauvaises, au contraire. Mais il m'a fallu quelques minutes pour ne penser à Lellouche, Lhermitte, Nakache et compagnie à chaque fois que l'un des personnages ouvrent la bouche.

Mention spéciale au premier épisode où c'est la première fois que je vois Astérix et Obélix enfants (hormis Obélix sur la couverture d'un album hors-série que je n'ai jamais lu), ils sont vraiment trop choupis. Il me semble qu'il y a également un autre album où Obélix est enfant, peut-être dans La Galère d'Obélix ? Bref, en tout cas, c'est la première fois en animation.

Mais ça passe trop vite, c'est trop dur. Alors je me suis forcé à ne pas aller plus loin et patienter 24-48h pour regarder les deux dernières parties.

Dommage que les mecs de TAT ne bossent pas pour le prochain long d'animation annoncé pour 2026 (Astérix et le Royaume de Nubie), après un rendu visuel aussi ébouriffant, ça va être dur de revenir à du “ classique ” (comme ceux d'Astier, très sympathiques au demeurant).

video_title: Asterix & Obelix: The Big Fight | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
I can't say I'm a fan of this one, having grown up with the original films. Seems they're taking it the same route they've taken the Smurfs: rather than make something timeless and distinct, it's all homogenised and americanised hyper-active cartoons of a cartoon. 

The Big Fight film where the soothsayer tricks the Gauls into abandoning their village was peak Asterix, imo: good soundtrack, solid action, and a lot darker than usual. This? This looks like Asterix in name only. Again, same as what's happened with the Smurfs, unfortunately. Over-exaggerated motions all around (though at least they don't all move like Jim Carrey's Grinch this time), so many modern references it might as well be a different franchise, it'll be dated in five years, as opposed to the originals which still hold up pretty well. The villain song from Mission Cleopatra alone is still up there with the greats. This film, though... not holding my breath. That's not even mentioning the choices of NPR techniques. Seriously, they couldn't even try to put an inverted hull on these things? No?
